description_long: This is a medium-sized cargo van with a boxy shape and a flat roof, painted in a faded green color with graffiti and spray-painted images on its sides. The van features two front doors, two rear doors, and likely a sliding side door, as is typical for this style. The windows are limited, with only the front windshield, two front side windows, and two small rear windows visible, all of which are darkened or tinted. The wheels are painted green and brown, and the van's body shows signs of wear, dirt, and urban street art, including images of animals, fire, and text. The front grille and headlights are simple and rectangular, with no visible manufacturer logo. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian vehicle, possibly used for deliveries or as a service van, but now repurposed or abandoned in an urban environment.
description: A green, graffiti-covered cargo van with minimal windows, boxy shape, and visible signs of wear, featuring animal and fire motifs on its sides.
description_view_0: Left side view showing graffiti art, including a bee, deer, and crossed-out text, with two front wheels and a side door seam visible.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying two rear doors with animal silhouettes and two small rear windows, as well as taillights and bumper details.
description_view_2: Right side view highlighting a deer, fire motif, and a single animal silhouette near the rear wheel, with the side door seam and wheels visible.
description_view_3: Front view showing a flat windshield, rectangular headlights, and a simple grille, with the hood and roof extending back.
attribute_door_configuration: two front doors, one sliding side door, two rear doors
